creativity_constraint: bridge physical sciences and life sciences

targets.title: Percolation Threshold Theory × T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors
bridge_concepts: LOX collagen crosslink density as bond occupation probability p
Percolation threshold p_c as immune exclusion threshold
Correlation length ξ ~ |p - p_c|^(-ν) predicts T cell clustering scale
Finite-size scaling of T cell MSD as proximity-to-threshold diagnostic
Universality class critical exponents (ν ≈ 0.88 in 3D) testable across tumor types
targets.impact_potential: HIGH — LOX is druggable (BAPN); percolation threshold predicts binary immune exclusion/infiltration switch; applicable across solid tumor types
risk_flags: Active T cell motility complicates passive percolation model

targets.title: Acoustic Filter-Bank Theory × Plant Bioacoustics
targets.field_a: Acoustic engineering — matched-filter detection, parallel filter-bank theory
targets.field_c: Plant biology — mechanosensitive channel biology, ultrasonic emission detection
bridge_concepts: Trichome arrays as tuned mechanical resonators (Euler-Bernoulli beam resonance)
MSL/MCA channel gating by specific acoustic frequencies coupled to resonant structures
Leaf surface microstructure as parallel filter-bank array
Acoustic impedance matching via trichome geometry (quarter-wave transformer analogy)
targets.impact_potential: MEDIUM — fundamental understanding of plant acoustic sensing; agricultural applications if frequency-selective response confirmed
risk_flags: Young field with limited quantitative data
Trichome resonance frequency may not match biologically relevant signals

targets.title: Classical Nucleation Theory × Ferroptosis Labile Iron Pool Dynamics
targets.field_a: Physics — classical nucleation theory, heterogeneous nucleation kinetics
targets.field_c: Cell death biology — ferroptosis, labile iron pool regulation
targets.disjointness_method: zero cross-field papers noted in S012
bridge_concepts: Ferritin iron core ferrihydrite dissolution as reverse heterogeneous nucleation
Critical nucleus size a* = 2γV_m/(kT·ln(S)) for LIP overflow
Nucleation rate J = A·exp(−ΔG*/kT) predicts threshold ferroptotic LIP burst
Ferritinophagy (NCOA4-mediated) as kinetic driver controlling supersaturation S
targets.impact_potential: HIGH — explains threshold behavior of ferroptosis; therapeutic targeting of nucleation barrier
risk_flags: Ferritin dissolution may follow non-CNT kinetics

targets.title: Linear Viscoelastic Creep Theory × Biofilm Antibiotic Tolerance
targets.field_a: Materials science — linear viscoelastic theory (Maxwell, Kelvin-Voigt models)
targets.field_c: Microbiology — biofilm antibiotic tolerance, persister formation
targets.disjointness_method: estimated — biofilm rheology exists but specific creep-compliance-to-MIC bridge is novel
bridge_concepts: Creep compliance J(t) as antibiotic penetration kinetics model
Retardation time τ determines pharmacokinetic shadow depth in biofilm
G'/G" crossover frequency predicts solid-like barrier vs liquid-like permeable matrix
MIC of biofilm-embedded bacteria correlates with EPS G'/G" ratio, not planktonic MIC
targets.impact_potential: HIGH — quantitative MIC prediction for biofilm infections; rheology-guided antibiotic dosing

failed_paradigm_details.why_it_failed: Cells are active matter with molecular motors; passive viscoelastic models fundamentally inadequate
failed_paradigm_details.why_it_works_here: Biofilm EPS matrices are PASSIVE crosslinked polymer gels without molecular motors — the abandoned framework is the correct physics

risk_flags: EPS may be nonlinear viscoelastic at large strains
Biofilm rheology subfield exists — verify specific bridge novelty

targets.title: Griffith Fracture Mechanics × Bacterial Cell Wall Failure Under β-Lactam Stress
targets.field_a: Materials science/engineering — Griffith fracture mechanics, stress intensity factors
targets.field_c: Microbiology — peptidoglycan mechanics, β-lactam mechanism of action
targets.disjointness_method: estimated — cell wall mechanics exists but Griffith criterion specifically not applied
bridge_concepts: PG mesh as pressurized thin-walled vessel under turgor (P = 2-5 atm)
β-Lactam-induced crosslink defects as cracks: K_I = σ√(πa) where σ = PR/t
Griffith criterion G ≥ 2γ for critical defect size predicting lysis threshold
Subcritical crack growth via autolysins at crack tips (stress corrosion analog)
Weakest-link (extreme value) statistics for lysis timing, not normal distribution
targets.impact_potential: HIGH — reframes antibiotic resistance as fracture toughness problem; predicts which PBP mutations most increase K_Ic
risk_flags: PG mesh spacing (~2 nm) may be too small for continuum fracture mechanics
Discrete lattice fracture models needed as backup

targets.title: Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy × Gut Microbiome Metabolic State
targets.field_c: Gut microbiology — microbiome metabolic activity, dysbiosis detection
targets.disjointness_method: estimated — no real-time functional gut microbiome monitoring tool exists
bridge_concepts: EIS frequency sweep as real-time metabolic fingerprint of microbial communities
Charge transfer resistance R_ct reflects microbial extracellular electron transfer rates
Nyquist plot topology tracks community metabolic shifts (healthy vs dysbiotic)
Warburg impedance captures mucus-layer diffusion limitation (dysbiosis marker)
targets.impact_potential: MEDIUM — real-time gut monitoring tool; early dysbiosis detection before compositional changes
risk_flags: Practical feasibility of EIS electrodes in gut environment

recommendation.rationale: Highest score (8.5), verified DISJOINT, quantitative physics with measurable biology, druggable control parameter (LOX/BAPN)
recommendation.exploration_rationale: failed_paradigm_recycling (0 sessions) with clean recycling logic and quantitative bridge
